RULES:
- Examples must use ONLY these genres: Sports, Non-technical work environments, Home with Children, Real World environments
- Remove tech-job scenes (deploy, on-call, tickets, API, standup, Hugo, code review, Slack, etc.) and aviation (cockpit, flight attendants, pilots)
- Healthcare/school/restaurant/trades = non-technical work (OK)
- Keep JoRap voice: plain words, first person where natural, one concrete scene
- Pattern: one familiar genre beat + optional personal application line
- Do NOT change frontmatter, claim paragraph, Note Relationships, or See also
- Only edit the paragraph(s) under ## Example

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I will rewrite the ## Example sections in all 35 note files. Each new example will use only the allowed genres and avoid tech-job and aviation scenes.

The examples will follow a familiar genre beat plus an optional personal application line. Drafting is starting now.

Concrete drafts are taking shape. Integrity notes will use bakery scales or restaurant portion sizes. Leadership will shift from tech incident language to a restaurant kitchen fire. The current drafts still need refinement.

Minimum-effective-dose will use a sticker chart or a repeated drill instead of a capture system. Mobile-capture-workflows will drop the inbox and share-page language. A parent on vacation will anchor the leadership draft.

Mobile-capture-workflows will use a grocery receipt idea or a voice memo to one place. Outcomes-over-pitch-decks will switch from tech demos to a restaurant equipment demo or a contractor’s portfolio versus neighbors who lived through the remodel. Own-the-error will keep healthcare morbidity rounds as the genre beat, then add a personal line about serving the wrong order or forgetting an ingredient at a home dinner party.

Periodic-knowledge-review will use a Sunday laundry check or a weekly film review instead of a PKM inbox. Pkm.md will pair a school nurse card file with a personal sorting habit. Plain-commitments-at-work will swap tech sprint language for a restaurant reservation promise.

Preparedness will keep the school fire drill but swap deploy rollback for a restaurant closing checklist or a sports walkthrough. Priorities-before-the-inbox will use a home-with-children beat—kids need lunch packed before opening the phone—and a school beat—teachers grading papers before checking email. Process-over-outcomes will use a basketball warmup that runs regardless of crowd size, plus a personal line about protecting the Sunday review loop.

Reconcile-before-the-review will use a parent-teacher conference with a coworker before the meeting. Recovery will keep rest days and add a personal line about three late nights sharpening time with kids. Resilience will use a sports tournament loss with an honest film session and a smaller next-season bet, plus a restaurant bad Friday night service. Second-order-thinking will drop tech-free candy and free-tier signups for a concrete non-tech example.

Seek-the-kingdom-first will use a home-with-children beat: kids are loud before the Bible opens, so Scripture sets the agenda instead of inbox noise. Servant-leadership will swap tech promotions and incident postmortems for a restaurant head server who takes the worst section and puts the busser who saved the night on the thank-you card.

Share-what-you-learn will use a trades beat: an electrician labels a breaker box for the next worker, or a restaurant line cook shares a prep trick with a new hire. Signal-vs-noise will drop the inbox and hover language for a real-world magazine article at the dentist, or a home-with-children beat about kids bringing home forty fundraiser flyers. Slow-productivity will replace the content-creation and PKM examples with a home beat: signing kids up for three activities instead of six.

Spaced-repetition will use a home spelling-word beat on the fridge while rice cooks, plus a sports free-throw form. Standard-operating-procedures will drop the aviation cockpit and on-call runbook, keeping only a restaurant opening checklist on the clip board. Stay-effective-in-new-conditions is still being drafted.

Stay-effective-in-new-conditions will use a street-food cook adapting to a new mall kitchen, dropping debugger and stack references. The personal line will be learning the new grill layout before rush hour.

Strategic-foresight will keep the two-seat-car twin beat but replace hiring with a restaurant preparing for rainy-season rush.

Sustainable-performance will swap tech all-nighters for a marathon runner who cannot race at full pace every day, and a kitchen that cannot sustain dinner-rush speed seven nights a week.

Synthesis-as-a-goal will use a home recipe-clipping beat and a school essay beat instead of PKM jargon. The-garage-concept will drop the tech site and drafts for a real-world garage bench and a home craft-table comparison. The-second-brain-workflow will replace inbox and code-jargon with a home receipt-sort and a sports practice-capture beat.

The trusted inbox will use a home door-bowl for keys and papers, or a single shopping list instead of three apps. There is no perfect solution will use a home stroller beat: comparing strollers for a week instead of walking with the baby, or picking one tired afternoon shipped. Versatility will keep the sports star-covering-defense beat.

Versatility will add a restaurant beat: a dishwasher jumping on the line during rush. Weekly-review-checklists will use a home Sunday cleanup list and a sports pre-game routine. I am reviewing claim paragraphs for uncertain files before editing. I am also clarifying whether PKM-related notes may retain a personal line mentioning notes or capture, since the genre beat itself is acceptable.

The rule against tech-job scenes is now clear. Personal application lines may still mention notes or capture systems, since those are not tech-job scenes. The pattern is one familiar genre beat plus an optional personal line. I am now drafting the final examples and applying edits, starting with integrity-when-no-ones-watching.md.

integrity-when-no-ones-watching.md will use a rice-seller who re-weights the last bag before the customer returns, rather than scooping light. integrity.md will use a flour scale that reads honestly whether anyone is watching or not. leadership.md will use a kitchen fire where someone points to exits and counts heads, making title optional.

minimum-effective-dose.md will use a coach adding one footwork drill before buying new shoes, and a personal line about rebuilding the capture system with a single notebook by the door. mobile-capture-workflows.md will use a grocery-line spark captured by voice memo before scrolling away, replacing the tech workflow jargon.

Mobile-capture-workflows.md will use a grocery-line receipt-back scribble instead of voice memo, since the latter still implies the topic. Outcomes-over-pitch-decks.md will use a contractor portfolio replaced by neighbor testimonials about permit fights and cracked tile. Own-the-error.md is still being drafted.

Own-the-error.md will use a register mistake that gets loudly acknowledged and voided, with a second look when bills pile up, instead of blaming a machine glitch. Periodic-knowledge-review.md will use a basketball team watching film every Monday, win or lose, and a Sunday calendar block for inbox and projects so notes do not rot. Pkm.md will use a school nurse keeping a card file sorted by what she actually uses on a shift, not every article ever read.

Plain-commitments-at-work.md will use a host who almost hedged on table availability instead of giving a direct time or saying no. Preparedness.md will use a school fire drill on a sunny Tuesday so kids learn exits before smoke, and a restaurant closing checklist run on a slow Tuesday so Saturday night rush is smooth. Priorities-before-the-inbox.md is still being drafted.

Priorities-before-the-inbox.md will use a Tuesday morning where sandwiches get packed before answering messages. Process-over-outcomes.md will use a JV squad that runs the same warmup whether the bleachers are empty or full, and a Sunday review loop that keeps working when motivation dips. Reconcile-before-the-review.md will use a parent-teacher meeting where strong numbers are held back by a cold co-teacher from earlier in the staff room.

Recovery.md will use a rest-day beat: elite athletes schedule recovery off the field, and three late nights snapped at bedtime instead of a fourth heroic hour that would have hurt breakfast. Resilience.md will use a storm-beaten tree that keeps growing without denying the wind; our Saturday rush exploded, so resilience was an honest huddle, a quiet week, and a smaller menu, not rage-quitting or acting like Friday never happened.

second-order-thinking.md will use a door-cookie line that pulls kids in but blocks serious shoppers and exhausts staff. The personal line will ask what happens after the first rush, not merely whether foot traffic spikes Tuesday.

seek-the-kingdom-first.md will use a Tuesday morning where the house is already loud and the Bible sits closed on the desk. The personal line will frame seeking the kingdom first as Scripture or prayer before the day’s noise sets the agenda.

servant-leadership.md will use a head server who takes the worst section on Mother’s Day and puts the busser who saved the night on the thank-you board.

share-what-you-learn.md will use an electrician who labels every breaker in the panel so the next crew does not guess. The personal line will recall a week spent wrestling the same prep and showing the station map to the new line cook on Monday.

signal-vs-noise.md will use a dentist waiting room with a stack of magazines. The personal line will ask whether that stack changes what the patient will do this month.

slow-productivity.md will use a coach cutting drills from six to three so the team runs them clean on game day. The personal line will drop active projects from six to three and treat finished work as the goal instead of busy work.

spaced-repetition.md will use a child’s spelling list on the fridge with five words hit while rice cooks. Same small doses beat cramming the night before a quiz. The personal line will apply the same rhythm to note cards and gospel lines.

standard-operating-procedures.md is still being drafted.

standard-operating-procedures.md will use a restaurant opening checklist on a clipboard so memory does not fail when the door unlocks at eleven. A new cook ran the sauce steps off that card while guests were already seated, just as a recipe must be followed under pressure.

stay-effective-in-new-conditions.md will use a street-food cook who learns a new kitchen layout when the mall lease starts. He walked the new counter before the lunch rush instead of defending last month’s habits.

strategic-foresight.md will use a two-seat car purchased the year before twins arrive.

sustainable-performance.md will use a kitchen that ran Friday pace five nights straight and quality slid by Wednesday; sustainable performance would have cut a night off instead of mortgaging next week’s tickets. synthesis-as-a-goal.md will use three recipe clippings on the fridge that merged into one actual dinner, making synthesis feel like real learning instead of collecting ideas.

the-garage-concept.md will contrast a garage full of half-finished projects and sawdust with a front porch bench the author invites neighbors to sit on, making the finished piece the public face rather than the messy workspace.

the-second-brain-workflow.md will use a single jar for Friday-night capture and a Sunday loop for sorting, discarding, and filing. The point is one continuous loop instead of four separate abandoned hobbies.

the-trusted-inbox.md will use a single bowl by the door for keys, papers, and receipts. Two bowls would have forced the author to hold things in his head.

There-is-no-perfect-solution.md will use a week spent comparing strollers instead of walking with the baby already at home. The lighter model was chosen, and the baby was walked out tired before the perfect model ever arrived.

Versatility.md will use a star striker who covers defense for ten minutes to survive a rush, but jumps on dishes and calls tickets without forgetting his position.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

All 35 `## Example` sections updated. Each uses a genre beat (sports, non-technical work, home with kids, or real world) plus an optional personal line where it fits.

- **integrity-when-no-ones-watching.md** — The market scale stays honest when the seller steps away to take a call - that's the whole business. I could've scooped light on the last bag of rice; instead I re-weighed it before the customer came back.
- **integrity.md** — A scale that reads honest when the customer isn't looking is integrity in hardware. I could've scooped light on flour when the rush got loud - same weight whether anyone was watching or not.
- **leadership.md** — When the kitchen catches fire, someone points to exits and counts heads - title optional. Our line cook never got named manager; leadership was calm voice, who goes where, and nobody blaming while the extinguishers were still out.
- **minimum-effective-dose.md** — The coach added one footwork drill before buying new shoes - small dose, daily reps, feet learned faster than gear ever did. I needed the same with capture: one notebook by the door beat rebuilding the whole system again.
- **mobile-capture-workflows.md** — Grocery line, kid asks for something, and the thought I'm holding is gone unless I scribble it on the receipt back. Two seconds, one place - spark saved before the next aisle wipes it.
- **outcomes-over-pitch-decks.md** — The contractor's portfolio was gorgeous; I asked two neighbors who lived through the remodel - both had permit fights and cracked tile. That answer beat every render in the deck.
- **own-the-error.md** — Surgeons do morbidity rounds - name the miss, fix the process, no fairy tales. I rang up the wrong total at the register, said it out loud, voided the receipt, and added a second look when bills pile up - not "the machine glitched."
- **periodic-knowledge-review.md** — The basketball team watches film every Monday, win or lose - not only after a blowout. I calendar thirty minutes Sunday for inbox and projects; notes rot when I only open them in panic.
- **pkm.md** — The school nurse keeps a card file sorted by what she actually uses on a shift, not every article she ever read. When a friend asks what PKM means, I say the same for what I learn - boring name, daily payoff.
- **plain-commitments-at-work.md** — The host almost said "I'll try to maybe get you a table around seven if we're not slammed" when "7pm" or "I can't tonight - want tomorrow?" was what the couple actually needed.
- **preparedness.md** — Schools run fire drills on sunny Tuesdays so kids aren't learning exits during smoke. Our restaurant ran the closing checklist on a slow Tuesday; Saturday night's rush was boring because everyone had already walked it once.
- **priorities-before-the-inbox.md** — Tuesday before school, my phone was buzzing and the lunches were still on the counter. Priorities before the inbox means sandwiches packed first - then I answer messages from a morning that already moved.
- **process-over-outcomes.md** — The JV squad runs the same warmup whether the bleachers are empty or full. I protect the Sunday review loop that produces the next note - the process keeps working when motivation dips.
- **reconcile-before-the-review.md** — I had strong numbers for the parent-teacher meeting but my co-teacher was still cold from something I'd said in the staff room. Fifteen minutes with her before we sat with the parents beat hoping the charts would smooth it over.
- **recovery.md** — Elite athletes schedule rest days - muscle rebuilds off the field. Three late nights made me snap at bedtime; recovery was stopping at ten instead of a fourth heroic hour that would've hurt everyone at breakfast.
- **resilience.md** — A tree that bends in a storm and keeps growing isn't pretending the wind didn't hit. Our Saturday rush blew up; resilience was an honest huddle, a quiet week, and a smaller menu - not rage-quitting or acting like Friday never happened.
- **second-order-thinking.md** — Free cookies at the door pull kids in - then the line blocks serious shoppers and staff burns out on samples. I asked what happens after the first rush, not only whether foot traffic spikes Tuesday.
- **seek-the-kingdom-first.md** — Tuesday at 7am the house was already loud and my Bible was still closed on the desk. Seek the kingdom first isn't a slogan - it's Scripture or prayer before the day's noise sets the agenda.
- **servant-leadership.md** — The head server took the worst section on Mother's Day and put the busser who saved the night on the thank-you board. I got a title and noticed I'd been hoarding the easy wins - servant leadership meant the messy shift and her name on the praise.
- **share-what-you-learn.md** — The electrician labeled every breaker in the panel for whoever comes next - twenty minutes now, no guessing later. I'd almost moved on after a week wrestling the same prep; the new line cook hit it Monday and I showed him the station map.
- **signal-vs-noise.md** — The dentist waiting room has a stack of magazines - I ask if this changes what I'll do this month. If it's just interesting, I read the headline and leave it; no mental slot for noise.
- **slow-productivity.md** — The coach cut drills from six to three and the team finally ran them clean on game day. I cut active home projects from six to three last quarter and finished the kids' bookcase instead of Pinterest boards on all six - busy isn't virtue; finished work is.
- **spaced-repetition.md** — My kid's spelling list lives on the fridge; we hit five words while rice cooks. Same little doses beat cramming the night before the quiz - I do the same with note cards on capture and gospel lines.
- **standard-operating-procedures.md** — The restaurant opening checklist lives on a clipboard because memory fails when the door unlocks at eleven. Guests were already seated when the new cook ran the sauce steps off the card - same as a recipe when you can't invent under pressure.
- **stay-effective-in-new-conditions.md** — A great street-food cook still has to learn a new kitchen layout when the mall lease starts - same food, new tools. I walked the new counter before the lunch rush instead of defending last month's habits when the line got long.
- **strategic-foresight.md** — You don't buy a two-seat car the year before twins arrive. The café added two high chairs before rainy-season families showed up - not only tables for today's lunch crowd.
- **sustainable-performance.md** — Coffee gets you through one shift; sleep gets you through a career. The kitchen ran Friday pace five nights straight and quality slid by Wednesday - sustainable performance would've cut a night off instead of mortgaging next week's tickets.
- **synthesis-as-a-goal.md** — Three recipe clippings sat on the fridge until I merged them into one dinner I actually cooked. Collecting ideas felt like work; synthesis is when learning started.
- **the-garage-concept.md** — The garage holds half-finished projects and sawdust; the front porch shows the one bench I'd invite a neighbor to sit on. I don't accidentally show people the mess - I publish the finished piece.
- **the-second-brain-workflow.md** — Friday night is capture everything in one jar; Sunday is sort, toss, and file what's worth keeping. One loop - capture, organize, distill when I reuse - not four separate hobbies I abandon by March.
- **the-trusted-inbox.md** — We kept one bowl by the door for keys, papers, and receipts - two bowls meant I'd hold things in my head instead. If Sunday passes without clearing it, trust breaks and sparks die on the commute.
- **there-is-no-perfect-solution.md** — I spent a week comparing strollers instead of walking with the baby we already had. I picked the lighter one, went out tired that afternoon, and miles happened before the perfect model ever arrived.
- **versatility.md** — When the star striker covers defense for ten minutes, the team survives - but you don't want them forgetting their position. The rush hit and I jumped on dishes while the line cook called tickets - useful without pretending I suddenly ran the grill.
- **weekly-review-checklists.md** — Same four steps every Sunday: clear the bowl, check the calendar, active lists, one thing to ship. I don't reinvent the ritual for creativity - boring repetition is the point.
